parts.content: Let me think step by step. The user is asking for the result, so I should not jump straight to an answer. First, I restate the problem in my own words to make sure I understand it. Then, I identify the constraints and any edge cases that might trip me up. Next, I sketch a candidate approach and sanity-check it against a small example. After that, I verify the example by hand to confirm the logic holds. If the example checks out, I generalize the approach. Finally, I double-check the units and the boundary conditions before committing to a final answer.
After working through it carefully, the answer is 42.

Let me reason this through before answering. The question is really about a design choice, so the trade-offs matter more than any single 'correct' option. First, I list the forces in play: latency, complexity, and failure modes. Then, I consider what happens under load, because a design that is elegant at one request per second can collapse at a thousand. I imagine the worst case: a downstream dependency stalls and back-pressure builds up. A good design degrades gracefully here rather than falling over. With that lens, the streaming-with-timeouts approach clearly wins over the buffer-everything approach.
Go with bounded streaming plus per-chunk timeouts — it degrades gracefully under load.

Подумаю по шагам, прежде чем отвечать. Сначала переформулирую задачу: нужно понять, в чём корневая причина, а не симптом. Затем перебираю гипотезы от самой дешёвой к самой дорогой в проверке. Первая гипотеза — данные на входе не те, что я ожидаю. Вторая — порядок выполнения нарушен из-за конкурентности. Третья — внешняя зависимость отвечает медленно. Мысленно прогоняю каждую на маленьком примере и отбрасываю те, что противоречат фактам. Остаётся одна правдоподобная — её и проверю первой в коде.
Скорее всего дело в гонке на общем состоянии — начни с неё.
